note: Single source of truth for programmatically generated /compare/* pages. Slugs here MUST NOT collide with hand-authored pages already in public/compare/ (openpipe, predibase, together, bedrock-distill, proxis, ollama, openai, openai-fine-tune, rag). Every proof_href must resolve on disk under public/ — the generator + scripts/audit-seo-pages.cjs hard-fail on a dangling proof. Numeric claims rendered on a page MUST reuse an X04-fixtured claim substring (data/x04-claim-fixtures.json) so they cannot drift from the measured artifact.

competitors.name: Modal
competitors.positioning: Modal is a serverless GPU platform: you rent ephemeral GPU containers per second for training and inference jobs.
competitors.bluf: Modal rents you serverless GPUs by the second; kolm compiles your captured traffic into a signed model you own and run on any hardware. Pick Modal when you want pure on-demand compute with no model ownership. Pick kolm when you want a portable, verifiable artifact and a per-token bill that drops to zero after compile.

matrix.them: A serverless GPU container you rent
matrix.why: kolm's output outlives the session; Modal's does not.

matrix.kolm: Zero per-token after compile (you run the weights)
matrix.them: Per-second GPU billing for every run
matrix.why: Compiled weights have no metered inference bill.

matrix.kolm: GGUF / EXL2 / vLLM — runs on laptop, VPC, or air-gap
matrix.why: kolm exports a standard artifact with no runtime dependency on us.

matrix.kolm: Ed25519-signed receipt per compile, replayable at /verify-prod
matrix.them: Job logs, no cryptographic artifact receipt
matrix.why: kolm's receipt is the proof of what was built.

matrix.kolm: OpenAI-compatible gateway captures (input,output) pairs into your lake
matrix.why: kolm makes the training set a by-product of normal traffic.

matrix.kolm: Inline redaction on the capture path before storage
matrix.why: kolm scrubs PII on the way in by default.

matrix.kolm: Built-in: DeepSeek-R1 32B to 17.9 GB INT4 in 125.3 s on one RTX 5090
matrix.them: You script your own quantize step
matrix.why: kolm ships the measured quantize path; see the verified matrix.
kolm is open source end to end.

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose Modal when your workload is pure ephemeral batch compute (rendering, ETL, one-off training) and you do not want to own or serve a model afterward. Modal's per-second autoscaling is excellent for spiky, stateless GPU jobs.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when you make repeated LLM calls you already pay per token for, and you want to turn that traffic into a model you own, quantize to your own hardware, and stop paying per token — with a signed receipt for every build.
competitors.proof_stat_context: DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-32B compresses to 17.9 GB INT4 in 125.3 s on a single RTX 5090 in the verified quantize matrix.

faqs.q: Can I move off Modal to kolm without rewriting my app?
faqs.a: Yes. kolm exposes an OpenAI-compatible gateway, so you change the base URL and keep your existing client. The gateway captures (input, output) pairs as you go, which become the training set for a compiled specialist model.

faqs.q: Does kolm replace my GPU provider?
faqs.a: No. kolm is the compiler, not the cloud. You can compile on a rented Modal GPU, on a local RTX 5090, or in your own VPC. The output is a portable artifact you run anywhere.
Yes. kolm's output is a single signed .kolm file containing your quantized weights plus a receipt. There is no runtime dependency on kolm and no per-token bill behind it.

competitors.name: Fireworks AI
competitors.positioning: Fireworks AI is a hosted inference platform offering fast serving of open and fine-tuned models behind an API.
competitors.bluf: Fireworks serves models fast behind its own API and bills per token; kolm compiles a model you own and run with no per-token bill. Pick Fireworks for managed, low-latency hosted inference. Pick kolm when you want to own the weights, run them on your own hardware, and verify every build with a signed receipt.

matrix.why: kolm produces a portable artifact; Fireworks hosts.
Owned weights have no metered inference.
kolm hands you the model file, not a URL.

matrix.kolm: Council-distill from captured traffic to a 7B specialist
matrix.why: kolm turns live traffic into the training set.

matrix.kolm: INT4 / GGUF / EXL2 with a measured matrix
matrix.why: kolm exposes the quantize ladder and the numbers.
kolm ships a quality contract into the pipeline.

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose Fireworks when you want the lowest-friction managed inference for popular open models and are happy to keep paying per token for the operational simplicity of a hosted endpoint.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when your token bill is the cost you want to eliminate: compile your repeated workload into an owned model, quantize to your hardware, and serve it yourself with a verifiable build receipt.
competitors.proof_stat_context: The trinity-500 distilled 7B specialist asks the right clarifying question 96.5% of the time at 1.24 s mean latency in the published benchmark.

faqs.q: Is kolm slower than a hosted endpoint?
faqs.a: kolm produces a quantized model you serve yourself, so throughput depends on your hardware. The published matrix records 11.5 tok/s for a 32B INT4 model on one RTX 5090; smaller specialists are faster. The trade is metered cloud latency for owned, fixed-cost local serving.

faqs.q: Can I compile a model trained on my Fireworks traffic?
faqs.a: Point your existing client at the kolm gateway instead. It captures (input, output) pairs and PII-redacts on the way in. Those pairs become the distillation set for an owned specialist.

faqs.q: What do I get at the end?
faqs.a: A single .kolm artifact with quantized weights, the original bf16 SHA-256 hashes, and an Ed25519-signed receipt you can replay at /verify-prod.

competitors.name: Baseten
competitors.positioning: Baseten is a model deployment platform built around Truss packaging and managed autoscaling inference.
competitors.bluf: Baseten packages and autoscales your model deployments in its cloud; kolm compiles a signed, portable model you own and run anywhere. Pick Baseten for managed deployment ergonomics. Pick kolm when ownership, portability, and a verifiable build receipt matter more than a hosted control plane.

matrix.why: kolm's artifact is yours to move.

matrix.kolm: Gateway captures traffic into a lake
matrix.why: kolm makes the dataset a by-product.

matrix.kolm: Measured INT4 ladder (61.0 GB to 17.9 GB)
Runs with no network at inference

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose Baseten when you want a polished managed-deployment experience with Truss packaging and autoscaling, and you do not need to physically own and relocate the model artifact.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when you need a model you can carry into a VPC or an air-gapped environment, with a signed receipt proving exactly what was compiled.
competitors.proof_stat_context: The verified matrix records a 61.0 GB bf16 DeepSeek-R1 32B compressing to 17.9 GB INT4 on one consumer GPU.

faqs.q: Does kolm package models like Truss?
faqs.a: kolm's package is the .kolm artifact: quantized weights plus a signed receipt and the original weight hashes. It loads in standard runtimes (llama.cpp, vLLM, ExLlamaV2) without a kolm dependency.

faqs.q: Can I deploy a kolm artifact to my own cloud?
faqs.a: Yes. kolm deploy pushes an artifact to a device or VPC you control. There is no kolm control plane in the inference path.

faqs.q: How do I verify a build?
faqs.a: Run kolm verify on the artifact, or replay the receipt CID at /verify-prod. It checks the SHA-256 weight chain, the Ed25519 signature, and the K-score.

competitors.name: Replicate
competitors.positioning: Replicate runs open-source models behind a hosted API where you pay per prediction.
competitors.bluf: Replicate runs community models behind a pay-per-prediction API; kolm compiles your traffic into a model you own with no per-prediction bill. Pick Replicate for instant access to a catalog of hosted models. Pick kolm when you want to own and quantize a specialist trained on your own workload.

matrix.why: kolm's output is a file you keep.

matrix.kolm: INT4 in 125.3 s for a 32B model

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose Replicate when you want one-line access to a huge catalog of community models for prototyping or low-volume use, with zero infrastructure to manage.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when a specific high-volume workload dominates your bill and you want to compile it into an owned, quantized specialist with a verifiable receipt.
competitors.proof_stat_context: kolm quantized a 32B reasoning model to INT4 in 125.3 s on a single RTX 5090 in the verified matrix.

faqs.q: Can kolm import a Replicate model?
faqs.a: kolm compiles open-weight base models and distills specialists from your captured traffic. It does not re-host closed catalog endpoints; instead you point your client at the kolm gateway and build from your own pairs.
Yes, kolm has a free self-serve tier. Sign up to obtain an API key and compile your first artifact.
Compiling the same inputs on the same hardware yields identical output hashes, and the receipt makes that auditable.

competitors.name: Lamini
competitors.positioning: Lamini is an enterprise LLM platform focused on fine-tuning and memory-tuning hosted models.
competitors.bluf: Lamini fine-tunes hosted enterprise models; kolm compiles captured traffic into an owned, quantized specialist with a signed receipt. Pick Lamini for a managed enterprise fine-tuning workflow. Pick kolm when you want the trained model as a portable artifact you run yourself with verifiable provenance.

matrix.why: kolm makes data a by-product of usage.
Owned weights are free to run.

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose Lamini when you want a fully managed enterprise fine-tuning service and prefer to consume the result as a hosted endpoint rather than own the weights.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when ownership and portability of the tuned model are requirements — for example to deploy into a customer VPC or an air-gapped network with a signed build receipt.
competitors.proof_stat_context: The distilled 7B trinity-500 specialist answers at 1.24 s mean latency in the published benchmark.

faqs.a: kolm's primary path is council distillation from captured traffic into a small specialist, then quantization. The result is an owned model rather than a tuned hosted endpoint.

faqs.q: Can I keep the data in my VPC?
faqs.a: Yes. kolm is self-hostable end to end (Apache-2.0). Capture, distill, and compile can all run inside your own environment.

faqs.q: What proves the model is what I expect?
faqs.a: Every compile emits a signed receipt with the K-score and weight hashes, replayable at /verify-prod.

competitors.name: LM Studio
competitors.positioning: LM Studio is a desktop app for downloading and running GGUF models locally.
competitors.bluf: LM Studio downloads and runs existing GGUF models on your desktop; kolm compiles a specialist trained on your own traffic and signs the build. Pick LM Studio to run off-the-shelf models locally. Pick kolm when you want to produce the GGUF — distilled to your workload and quantized with a verifiable receipt.

matrix.why: kolm emits the artifact LM Studio can then run.
kolm runs the quantize step itself.

matrix.kolm: Capture to distill to compile to serve
matrix.why: kolm is the full build chain.

matrix.kolm: Output runs in LM Studio too

matrix.why: Both are accessible; kolm is open source.

competitors.when_them_wins: Choose LM Studio when you just want to chat with an existing open model locally with a friendly desktop UI and no build step.
competitors.when_kolm_wins: Choose kolm when you want to produce the model in the first place — a specialist distilled from your traffic and quantized to a GGUF you can then open in LM Studio, with a signed receipt of how it was built.
competitors.proof_stat_context: kolm compiles a 32B reasoning model to a 17.9 GB INT4 GGUF — small enough for a single consumer GPU — in 125.3 s.

faqs.q: Can I run a kolm artifact in LM Studio?
faqs.a: Yes. kolm exports standard GGUF, which LM Studio loads directly. kolm is the compiler; LM Studio is one of several runtimes for the output.

faqs.q: Why compile instead of downloading a model?
faqs.a: A downloaded model is generic. A kolm-compiled specialist is distilled from your own captured traffic, so it is smaller and tuned to your task, with a receipt that proves how it was built.
Quantization benefits from a GPU, but the resulting GGUF runs on CPU offload in LM Studio. The verified matrix figures were measured on a single RTX 5090.
